steps.title: Classify the function
steps.content: $f(x)=2.93x$ has the form $f(x)=mx+b$ with $m=2.93$ and $b=0$. That is a linear function (constant rate of change), not exponential. Because the slope $m=2.93\gt0$, the function is increasing.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) Decreasing exponential: $2.93x$ is not exponential, and its outputs grow as $x$ grows. ✗
  • B) Decreasing linear: linear is right, but the slope $2.93$ is positive, so $f$ increases, not decreases. ✗
  • C) Increasing exponential: $2.93x$ is a straight line, not a curve of the form $a\cdot b^x$. ✗
  • D) Increasing linear: linear with positive slope $2.93$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
